div.head: The Ambassador in the Dominican Republic (Warren) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 79. I called on Trujillo this morning and informed him orally of contents of Department’s telegram 59.See footnote 9, above. His response was immediate and cordial. In the absence of former [Minister] Franco,Tomás Hernández Franco, Dominican Minister to Haiti. who having been recalled is now in this country, and in view of fact his successorTulio Franco y Franco. has not yet proceeded to his post, Trujillo called Haitian MinisterAndré Chevallier, Haitian Minister in the Dominican Republic. to Presidency this afternoon and handed him a letter addressed to President LescotDated February 9; copy transmitted to the Department in despatch 1552, February 9, from Ciudad Trujillo (neither printed). inviting latter to meet him at frontier to discuss proposal of erecting peace monument in connection with Centennial program. He requested Chevallier to deliver letter personally and immediately to Lescot. Translation of letter follows in my next telegram.Telegram 81, February 9, 7 p.m., not printed.
